danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites in palace and tower rooms
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats; area requires combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Vertical scrolling tower rooms with boss chambers
Hidden portal locations throughout palace perimeter
danger_signals.meaning: Concealed danger; exploration risk of unknown encounters

danger_signals.visual_form: Complex multi-room structure in oblique perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Primary danger zone; main antagonist domain

safety_signals.meaning: Safe trading zones; equipment upgrade available

safety_signals.visual_form: Horizontally scrolling room with central flame feature
safety_signals.meaning: Safe sanctuary; mission objective location; ritual site

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific discovery rooms within maze structures

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting position outside palace; upgradeable equipment reflects progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shadowy water spirit; divine authority figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lake origin; mission-giver; represents kingdom protection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark antagonist; seven magic bells as power conduits
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss encounters in each of seven bell towers; final form in sanctum
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palace structure; tower dominance; bell artifact placement
Palace geography; threatened by Rubas's monster summoning army

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven intact bell towers visible in palace; Rubas's power active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters populate all areas; boss entities occupy tower summits; passage to Rubas blocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destroyed towers become inaccessible; sacred flame serves as state-change trigger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each destroyed bell removes one tower from the palace; decreases total monster density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final door opens revealing path to Rubas's sanctum
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss encounter becomes accessible; climactic confrontation enabled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Labyrinth state; chaotic palace structure; no safe zones except shops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Prince Myer weak at start; enemies numerous; resource scarcity (limited ludder drops)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipment upgrades manifest as visual changes to prince's offensive capabilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sword-throwing evolves from single projectile to multiple; enemy defeat speed increases

environmental_storytelling.example: 10 hidden dungeons → Palace exterior → 7 bell towers → Sacred flame → Final Rubas sanctum; communicates narrative escalation through geography
Rubas's palace labyrinth design signals evil dominance; sacred flame chamber opposes it as light/order counterpoint
Hidden dungeon entrances and secret parallel zones suggest Rubas's palace contains hidden corruption and alternate dark mirrors
Vertical scrolling in towers vs. horizontal scrolling in palace and sacred flame room; distinguishes threat zones by movement axis
Fixed shop locations in dungeons suggest merchant networks within/against the corrupted kingdom; ludder currency shows economic resistance to Rubas
Zelda-like gameplay requires backtracking through known areas as player grows stronger; world becomes re-readable as power increases

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Throws single sword; enemies take 8+ hits to defeat; slow combat effectiveness
Multiple projectile throwing; faster enemy defeat; indicates equipment acquisition
Populate all non-shop rooms; attack on contact; respawn behavior suggested by continuous population
Occupy tower summits; serve as progression gates; defeat required to collect bells
Accepts collected bells; consumes them to destroy towers; opens final passage when all seven processed
Absent from palace; presence inferred through monster summoning and tower guardianship; final encounter requires all bell destruction

notes: Deadly Towers communicates world-state primarily through spatial design and architectural progression rather than dynamic environmental changes. The game uses a rigid progression lock (bells → sacred flame → final boss door) that is purely visual/mechanical rather than state-based. The Japanese original 'Mashō' (Evil Bell) title and red LED cartridge feature reinforced the evil-bell danger signal. World-state is largely binary (bells intact vs. destroyed) with cumulative progression. Faction identity is minimal—primarily antagonist (Rubas) vs. player (Prince Myer) with no visible faction armies or territorial markers. The oblique isometric perspective provides consistent visual language for palace geography. Danger/safety distinction relies on room type and merchant presence rather than dynamic visual warnings.
